scene.title: Housewarming main room refresh
scene.summary: A housewarming where the main room will be photographed by guests and posted.

mcq_options.text: Blend half the private floor with half of what the weighted friends are doing, regardless of peer-sensitivity — arrives near $480.
Mirror the closest friend's level straight up, with no separate private-baseline term — near $552.
Cut the refresh budget in the opposite direction of the visible friends trend, treating peer pull as a reason to pull back — near $420.
Let each of the friends tug the refresh budget in proportion to how much weight that relationship carries, layered above the baseline need — near $510.

cot_text: Reasoning: This reads as a positional-consumption scene for the protagonist: the observable cues in the setting match that category. If it were positional-human-capital, then there is no study-hour or skill-signal race at stake, only a spend whose size is read socially, which is not what the scene actually shows. If it were non-positional, then the commitment is read off a shared social standard — peers' amounts are treated as a bar to meet, not just as other people's private situations, which likewise does not match the scene. Within this category, the commitment is lifted above the private baseline by a closeness-weighted reference drawn from the friends. The close-in-life relationships carry most of the weight; the more peripheral ones barely register. The comparison-sensitivity of the protagonist scales that aggregate pull before it stacks on top of the baseline floor. Gold rule: anchor on the baseline and lift by the closeness-weighted reference, giving a target refresh budget near $510.
